body.text: This paper establishes the philosophical foundations for cognitive liberty, arguing that the right to cognitive self-determination is a fundamental human right that must be protected in the age of AI and neurotechnology.

Building on disability rights frameworks and neurodiversity paradigms, this work establishes cognitive liberty as the principle that individuals have the right to control their own cognitive processes and mental autonomy.
Right to access and use cognitive prosthetics
Protection against cognitive surveillance and manipulation
All P31 Labs artifacts are designed to uphold cognitive liberty principles, ensuring users maintain sovereignty over their cognitive processes while benefiting from assistive technologies.
